name: Ceremonial gate (torii / paifang)
description: An East-Asian ceremonial gate: two pillars carrying a straight tie beam (nuki) and a curved upturned top lintel (kasagi) whose tips sweep up. Vermilion by default. Spans the longer horizontal axis of its plot; marks a main entrance or a garden threshold.
placement_rules: Straddles a path or the main approach axis to the building.
Thin on the cross axis (1-3 cells); spans the path on the long axis.
Pairs well with a cobble/path approach and a fountain or pond beyond.

japanese.notes: Shinto torii: vermilion posts and lintel, dark tie beam.

chinese.notes: Paifang: red posts with gilt beam.

python_skill_signature: pipeline.skills.torii_gate.build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
style_affinity: East-Asian (Japanese/Chinese) motif. Coherent in japanese/chinese (and fantasy). Out of place in Western, Gothic, Renaissance, Modern, Mediterranean buildings — use only if the building is East-Asian.
